tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and low specific gravity, indicating possible tubular dysfunction and/or glomerular damage. No evidence of urinary infection. Low HbA1c with glucosuria may suggest renal glycosuria or recent glycemic changes. Findings are consistent with significant renal impairment; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
